fragility_points: This is a narrow-source touch market with operationally specific settlement mechanics.
A broadly bullish SOL narrative can still fail to resolve Yes if Binance never prints 90.00+ on a 1-minute high.
Confidence is sensitive to short-horizon volatility and momentum persistence.
key_assumptions: SOL remains close enough to $90 that ordinary short-horizon volatility can generate a qualifying wick.
Binance will reflect any broader upside move in SOL during the remaining window.
Market conditions do not deteriorate enough to push SOL materially away from the threshold before expiry.
main_logical_chain: Polymarket contract text makes Binance SOL/USDT 1-minute highs the governing source of truth.
Binance data shows SOL already reached 89.15 during the relevant week and was still trading around 88.8-88.9 during analysis.
Because only a brief wick is needed, Yes is favored, but narrow venue-specific and timing risks justify a lower probability than the market's 74%.
main_thesis: SOL is more likely than not to touch $90 on Binance before April 19, but the market overstates confidence because narrow source-of-truth and timing/path risks remain material.
quote_anchors: This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1-minute candle for SOL/USDT ... has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title.
source_quality_view: Primary quality is solid because the contract text and Binance data directly match the settlement mechanics; independence is medium and contextual cross-checking is lighter than ideal.
strongest_disconfirmers: No checked Binance data in this run showed a qualifying 90+ print yet.
Only Binance SOL/USDT 1-minute highs count, so other-venue strength is irrelevant.
Round-number rejection and late-window volatility compression could keep SOL below the threshold.
strongest_supports: Binance, the governing venue, already printed a relevant-week high of 89.15.
Current spot checks were still in the high-88s, leaving less than 1% upside to trigger a Yes.
The contract only requires a 1-minute touch, not a sustained break or close above $90.
timing_relevance: High. The market resolves on a date-specific touch condition, so path and remaining-window volatility matter more than medium-term SOL fundamentals.
unresolved_ambiguities: Whether $90 acts as a brief wick target or a near-term ceiling.
How much volatility will remain in the final days before expiry.
Whether a stronger independent market-context source would shift the estimate by a few points.
what_would_change_view: I would move up if Binance highs start probing 89.5-89.9 or a direct 1-minute pass shows repeated 90 attempts; I would move down if SOL loses the high-88 area or momentum fades into expiry.
